mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: psychosis, chronic liver disease, abnormalities of the spleen and gastrointestinal tract (shuffling gait, hematochezia, chronic gastritis)
neurodegeneration with psychosis, splenomegaly, gastrointestinal symptoms (constipation, diarrhea), and immunological dysfunction
pancreas inflammation leading to jaundice, gastrointestinal symptoms, and possible involvement of other organs such as the bile ducts or salivary glands

mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.disease_name: Mental retardation, x-linked syndromic, Lubs type
mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: progressive destruction of small bile ducts causing cholestasis, pruritus, and potential complications including gonadal dysfunction
multiple café au lait spots, freckles, iris hamartomas, neurofibromas, axillary or inguinal fibrosis, scoliosis, shuffling gait, and possible neurological deficits
seizures, skin lesions (ash leaf macules, angiofibromas), mental retardation, renal anomalies, cardiac rhabdomyoma, and intestinal hamartomatous polyps
arthralgias, fever, weight loss, abdominal pain, steatorrhea, and central nervous system manifestations (confusion, dementia, myoclonus)

mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: malabsorption due to gluten sensitivity, resulting in chronic diarrhea, failure to thrive, and various extraintestinal features (anemia, osteoporosis, dermatitis herpetiformis, infertility)
lysosomal storage disorder characterized by accumulation of globotriaosylceramide, presenting with acroparasthesias, corneal opacifications, angiokeratomas, hypertension, and proteinuria

mucopolysaccharide storage disorder caused by defective processing of mannose-6-phosphate residues, featuring growth impairment, coarse facies, short stature, skeletal changes, hearing loss, and visceral organ enlargement
